mocca-emulator-start.template: Run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File '.\maestro-workspace\start-emulator.ps1'` and confirm emulator serial from `adb devices` output.

mocca-build.template: Run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "& '.\gradlew.bat' :androidApp:assembleDebug"` and summarize errors if any.

mocca-tests.template: Run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "& '.\gradlew.bat' :composeApp:allTests"` and report only failing tests with root causes.

mocca-e2e-smoke.template: Run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File '.\maestro-workspace\start-emulator.ps1'` first, then run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "& '.\maestro-workspace\run-emulator-tests.ps1' 'maestro-workspace/testplans/smoke.yaml' -BuildApk"`, and summarize failing steps with artifact paths.

mocca-logcat.template: Run `adb logcat -c && adb logcat *:W | findstr "mocca|Exception"` and summarize relevant errors.

mocca-agent-tooling.description: Check and bootstrap Android CLI, Android skills, and Android Knowledge Base integration
mocca-agent-tooling.template: Run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File 'C:\Users\ruzaq\AndroidStudioProjects\MOCCA\scripts\setup-android-agent-tooling.ps1'`. If Android CLI is missing, report the official download page and continue using the repo-native Gradle/Maestro commands.

mocca-android-describe.description: Describe MOCCA with Android CLI when available
mocca-android-describe.template: Run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File 'C:\Users\ruzaq\AndroidStudioProjects\MOCCA\scripts\android-cli.ps1' describe --project_dir='C:\Users\ruzaq\AndroidStudioProjects\MOCCA'`. Use the JSON artifact paths it returns to locate APK outputs and build targets.

mocca-android-docs.description: Search Android Knowledge Base before Android API or tooling changes
mocca-android-docs.template: Run `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File 'C:\Users\ruzaq\AndroidStudioProjects\MOCCA\scripts\android-cli.ps1' docs search '<task-specific query>'`, choose the most relevant `kb://` result, then run the same wrapper with `docs fetch '<kb://result>'`. Use fetched guidance over model memory for Android APIs, Compose, Gradle/AGP, R8, Play Billing, edge-to-edge, emulator, or performance work.
